title: AWS Weekend Agent Challenge: Daybreak
excerpt: Vision and what the agent does I build fast and I build a lot. As of this weekend that is...
content_html:
Vision and what the agent does

I build fast and I build a lot. As of this weekend that is 49 public repos. What I am bad at is remembering the state of any of them. Which ones are live, which shipped with no README, which went quiet months ago and quietly rotted. The answer used to be that I had no idea until I went digging.

Daybreak is the fix. I wanted the review done before I even sat down: not a dashboard I have to remember to check, but a brief already in my inbox, waiting at first light. That is the name. Every morning at 6 AM Pacific it wakes up on its own, with no button to press. It takes inventory of every repo on my GitHub account, reasons over that inventory with Amazon Nova, and leaves a short brief in my inbox: how many repos are live, how many have gone quiet, how many are missing a README or a license, and three specific "do something here" nudges. By the time I am awake, the review is done and waiting. The best tool is the one you never have to open.

It runs read-only. It reports, I decide. It never touches my repos.

How I built it

PRD first, always. One architecture doc with MUST, STUB, and NEVER labels before a line of code, then a block-by-block build with a pass or fail checkpoint after each block. Uptime checks, showcase scraping, and auto-generated README pull requests are all stubbed with implementation notes, not half-built. Shipping small and lean beat shipping big and broken.

I ran it as a multi-model workflow. Claude for the architecture and the PRD, Kiro for the spec-driven build. I wrote phase-aware guardrails so Kiro could do the file authoring and tests while I was at work, then flip to a go-live mode that let it deploy with my credentials once I got home. I direct and approve, the agents generate.

The challenge worth mentioning was getting Nova to answer at all. My first invoke failed with "on-demand throughput isn't supported." Nova will not run on-demand from the bare model id. You have to call the cross-region inference profile, us.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0, and your IAM policy has to allow both the profile and the underlying model in every region the profile routes to. List only the profile and you get AccessDenied even with model access on. Once I understood that, it answered first try.

A second lesson landed mid-build. The old Bedrock model access page was retired in late 2025 and Amazon-provided models are auto-enabled now, so a step I had planned no longer existed. Checking whether a tool is current before depending on it saved me from chasing a dead console page.

AWS services used and architecture overview

The whole thing is one Lambda and a schedule.

  • Amazon EventBridge Scheduler fires the function daily at 6 AM Pacific with a timezone-aware cron, so daylight saving handles itself. This is the trigger, not a button.
  • AWS Lambda runs the agent in Python with zero third-party dependencies, so the deploy package is a single zipped file.
  • Amazon Bedrock with Nova Lite via the Converse API does the reasoning.
  • Amazon SES emails the brief.
  • Amazon CloudWatch logs every run and alarms on any error.

Infrastructure is a single AWS SAM template, so the whole stack deploys and rolls back as one unit.

Flow: EventBridge Scheduler triggers Lambda, which reads the GitHub API, calls Bedrock Nova, and sends through SES to my inbox.

One design choice I am proud of: structure is deterministic, flavor is generated. The counts of how many repos are live, quiet, or unlicensed are computed in Python so they can never be hallucinated. Nova only writes the narrative and the nudges around those fixed numbers.

What I learned

Bedrock inference profiles, and why on-demand Nova needs the us. prefix and IAM that covers the fan-out regions. Least-privilege IAM for an agent: four actions, no wildcards, with Parameter Store wired for secrets (the token slot exists but v1 runs unauthenticated just fine). SES sandbox is fine when sender and recipient are the same verified address, which skips a production-access request. And the value of a hard line between deterministic logic and model reasoning, so the numbers are trustworthy and the writing is still warm.

The proof it ran without me: I set the EventBridge schedule to fire at 8:07 PM, stepped away, and it went off on its own. It pulled all 49 repos, Nova wrote the brief, and SES delivered it in about 4 seconds, with the CloudWatch timestamp and the email to match. That was a real scheduled trigger, not a button, which is the whole point of the challenge. With autonomy proven, I set the production schedule to 6 AM Pacific daily. The verbatim CloudWatch logs, timestamps, and request IDs are in PROOF.md.

Link to repo

Repo: github.com/earlgreyhot1701D/daybreak

Proof of autonomous run: PROOF.md

AI Assisted. Human Reviewed. Powered by NLP.

sentences.en: Vision and what the agent does
sentences.zh: 愿景和代理人的职责

blocks.html_en: I build fast and I build a lot. As of this weekend that is 49 public repos. What I am bad at is remembering the state of any of them. Which ones are live, which shipped with no README, which went quiet months ago and quietly rotted. The answer used to be that I had no idea until I went digging.

sentences.zh: 我建造得很快，而且建造得很多。

sentences.en: As of this weekend that is 49 public repos.
sentences.zh: 截至本周末，共有 49 个公共回购。

sentences.en: What I am bad at is remembering the state of any of them.
sentences.zh: 我不擅长的是记住他们中的任何一个人的状态。

sentences.en: Which ones are live, which shipped with no README, which went quiet months ago and quietly rotted.
sentences.zh: 哪些是实时的，哪些是没有自述文件的，几个月前就沉寂了，然后静静地腐烂了。

sentences.en: The answer used to be that I had no idea until I went digging.
sentences.zh: 过去的答案是我不知道，直到我去挖掘。

blocks.html_en: Daybreak is the fix. I wanted the review done before I even sat down: not a dashboard I have to remember to check, but a brief already in my inbox, waiting at first light. That is the name. Every morning at 6 AM Pacific it wakes up on its own, with no button to press. By the time I am awake, the review is done and waiting. The best tool is the one you never have to open.

sentences.en: Daybreak is the fix.
sentences.zh: 天一亮就解决了。

sentences.en: I wanted the review done before I even sat down: not a dashboard I have to remember to check, but a brief already in my inbox, waiting at first light.
sentences.zh: 我希望在坐下之前就完成审查：不是我必须记住检查的仪表板，而是我的收件箱中已经存在的摘要，等待黎明时分。

sentences.en: That is the name.
sentences.zh: 这就是名字。

sentences.en: Every morning at 6 AM Pacific it wakes up on its own, with no button to press.
sentences.zh: 太平洋时间每天早上 6 点，它会自行醒来，无需按任何按钮。

sentences.en: It takes inventory of every repo on my GitHub account, reasons over that inventory with Amazon Nova, and leaves a short brief in my inbox: how many repos are live, how many have gone quiet, how many are missing a README or a license, and three specific "do something here" nudges.
sentences.zh: 它会清点我的 GitHub 帐户上的每个存储库，通过 Amazon Nova 对该库存进行分析，并在我的收件箱中留下一个简短的说明：有多少个存储库处于活动状态，有多少个已安静，有多少个缺少自述文件或许可证，以及三个特定的“在此处执行某些操作”提示。

sentences.en: By the time I am awake, the review is done and waiting.
sentences.zh: 等我醒来的时候，审核已经完成，正在等待。

sentences.en: The best tool is the one you never have to open.
sentences.zh: 最好的工具是您永远不需要打开的工具。

blocks.html_en: It runs read-only. It reports, I decide. It never touches my repos.

sentences.en: It runs read-only.
sentences.zh: 它以只读方式运行。

sentences.en: It reports, I decide.
sentences.zh: 它报告了，我决定。

sentences.en: It never touches my repos.
sentences.zh: 它永远不会触及我的仓库。

blocks.html_en: How I built it

sentences.en: How I built it
sentences.zh: 我是如何构建它的

blocks.html_en: PRD first, always. One architecture doc with MUST, STUB, and NEVER labels before a line of code, then a block-by-block build with a pass or fail checkpoint after each block. Uptime checks, showcase scraping, and auto-generated README pull requests are all stubbed with implementation notes, not half-built. Shipping small and lean beat shipping big and broken.

sentences.en: PRD first, always.
sentences.zh: 珠三角始终第一。

sentences.en: One architecture doc with MUST, STUB, and NEVER labels before a line of code, then a block-by-block build with a pass or fail checkpoint after each block.
sentences.zh: 一个架构文档在一行代码之前带有 MUST、STUB 和 NEVER 标签，然后是逐块构建，每个块后面都有通过或失败检查点。

sentences.en: Uptime checks, showcase scraping, and auto-generated README pull requests are all stubbed with implementation notes, not half-built.
sentences.zh: 正常运行时间检查、展示抓取和自动生成的 README 拉取请求都带有实施说明，而不是半成品。

sentences.en: Shipping small and lean beat shipping big and broken.
sentences.zh: 小而精益的运输击败了大而破碎的运输。

blocks.html_en: I ran it as a multi-model workflow. Claude for the architecture and the PRD, Kiro for the spec-driven build. I wrote phase-aware guardrails so Kiro could do the file authoring and tests while I was at work, then flip to a go-live mode that let it deploy with my credentials once I got home. I direct and approve, the agents generate.

sentences.en: I ran it as a multi-model workflow.
sentences.zh: 我将其作为多模型工作流程运行。

sentences.en: Claude for the architecture and the PRD, Kiro for the spec-driven build.
sentences.zh: Claude 负责架构和 PRD，Kiro 负责规范驱动的构建。

sentences.en: I wrote phase-aware guardrails so Kiro could do the file authoring and tests while I was at work, then flip to a go-live mode that let it deploy with my credentials once I got home.
sentences.zh: 我编写了阶段感知护栏，以便 Kiro 可以在我工作时进行文件创作和测试，然后切换到上线模式，让它在我回家后使用我的凭据进行部署。

sentences.en: I direct and approve, the agents generate.
sentences.zh: 我指导并批准，代理人生成。

blocks.html_en: The challenge worth mentioning was getting Nova to answer at all. My first invoke failed with "on-demand throughput isn't supported." Nova will not run on-demand from the bare model id. You have to call the cross-region inference profile, us.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0, and your IAM policy has to allow both the profile and the underlying model in every region the profile routes to. List only the profile and you get AccessDenied even with model access on. Once I understood that, it answered first try.

sentences.en: The challenge worth mentioning was getting Nova to answer at all.
sentences.zh: 值得一提的挑战是让 Nova 做出回答。

sentences.en: My first invoke failed with "on-demand throughput isn't supported."
sentences.zh: 我的第一次调用失败，并显示“不支持按需吞吐量”。

sentences.en: Nova will not run on-demand from the bare model id.
sentences.zh: Nova 不会根据裸模型 ID 按需运行。

sentences.en: You have to call the cross-region inference profile, us.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0, and your IAM policy has to allow both the profile and the underlying model in every region the profile routes to.
sentences.zh: 您必须调用跨区域推理配置文件 us.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0，并且您的 IAM 策略必须允许配置文件路由到的每个区域中的配置文件和底层模型。

sentences.en: List only the profile and you get AccessDenied even with model access on.
sentences.zh: 仅列出配置文件，即使打开了模型访问权限，您也会收到 AccessDenied。

sentences.en: Once I understood that, it answered first try.
sentences.zh: 一旦我明白了这一点，它就回答了第一次尝试。

blocks.html_en: A second lesson landed mid-build. The old Bedrock model access page was retired in late 2025 and Amazon-provided models are auto-enabled now, so a step I had planned no longer existed. Checking whether a tool is current before depending on it saved me from chasing a dead console page.

sentences.en: A second lesson landed mid-build.
sentences.zh: 第二节课在构建中进行。

sentences.en: The old Bedrock model access page was retired in late 2025 and Amazon-provided models are auto-enabled now, so a step I had planned no longer existed.
sentences.zh: 旧的基岩模型访问页面已于 2025 年末停用，亚马逊提供的模型现在已自动启用，因此我计划的步骤已不复存在。

sentences.en: Checking whether a tool is current before depending on it saved me from chasing a dead console page.
sentences.zh: 在依赖某个工具之前检查它是否是最新的，这使我免于追逐死控制台页面。

blocks.html_en: AWS services used and architecture overview

sentences.en: AWS services used and architecture overview
sentences.zh: 使用的 AWS 服务和架构概述

blocks.html_en: The whole thing is one Lambda and a schedule.

sentences.zh: 整个事情就是一个 Lambda 和一个时间表。

blocks.html_en: • Amazon EventBridge Scheduler fires the function daily at 6 AM Pacific with a timezone-aware cron, so daylight saving handles itself. This is the trigger, not a button.

sentences.en: Amazon EventBridge Scheduler fires the function daily at 6 AM Pacific with a timezone-aware cron, so daylight saving handles itself.
sentences.zh: Amazon EventBridge Scheduler 每天早上 6 点（太平洋时间）通过时区感知的 cron 触发该函数，因此夏令时可以自行处理。

sentences.en: This is the trigger, not a button.
sentences.zh: 这是触发器，而不是按钮。

blocks.html_en: • AWS Lambda runs the agent in Python with zero third-party dependencies, so the deploy package is a single zipped file.

sentences.en: AWS Lambda runs the agent in Python with zero third-party dependencies, so the deploy package is a single zipped file.
sentences.zh: AWS Lambda 在 Python 中运行代理，第三方依赖项为零，因此部署包是单个压缩文件。

blocks.html_en: • Amazon Bedrock with Nova Lite via the Converse API does the reasoning.

sentences.en: Amazon Bedrock with Nova Lite via the Converse API does the reasoning.
sentences.zh: Amazon Bedrock 与 Nova Lite 通过 Converse API 进行推理。

blocks.html_en: • Amazon SES emails the brief.

sentences.en: Amazon SES emails the brief.
sentences.zh: Amazon SES 通过电子邮件发送简报。

blocks.html_en: • Amazon CloudWatch logs every run and alarms on any error.

sentences.en: Amazon CloudWatch logs every run and alarms on any error.
sentences.zh: Amazon CloudWatch 会记录每次运行并针对任何错误发出警报。

blocks.html_en: Infrastructure is a single AWS SAM template, so the whole stack deploys and rolls back as one unit.

sentences.en: Infrastructure is a single AWS SAM template, so the whole stack deploys and rolls back as one unit.
sentences.zh: 基础设施是单个 AWS SAM 模板，因此整个堆栈作为一个单元进行部署和回滚。

blocks.html_en: Flow: EventBridge Scheduler triggers Lambda, which reads the GitHub API, calls Bedrock Nova, and sends through SES to my inbox.

sentences.en: Flow: EventBridge Scheduler triggers Lambda, which reads the GitHub API, calls Bedrock Nova, and sends through SES to my inbox.
sentences.zh: 流程：EventBridge Scheduler 触发 Lambda，后者读取 GitHub API、调用 Bedrock Nova，并通过 SES 发送到我的收件箱。

blocks.html_en: One design choice I am proud of: structure is deterministic, flavor is generated. The counts of how many repos are live, quiet, or unlicensed are computed in Python so they can never be hallucinated. Nova only writes the narrative and the nudges around those fixed numbers.

sentences.en: One design choice I am proud of: structure is deterministic, flavor is generated.
sentences.zh: 我引以为傲的一个设计选择是：结构是确定性的，风味是生成的。

sentences.en: The counts of how many repos are live, quiet, or unlicensed are computed in Python so they can never be hallucinated.
sentences.zh: 活跃、安静或未经许可的存储库数量是用 Python 计算的，因此永远不会产生幻觉。

sentences.en: Nova only writes the narrative and the nudges around those fixed numbers.
sentences.zh: 诺瓦只围绕这些固定数字撰写叙述和推动。

blocks.html_en: What I learned

sentences.en: What I learned
sentences.zh: 我学到了什么

blocks.html_en: Bedrock inference profiles, and why on-demand Nova needs the us. prefix and IAM that covers the fan-out regions. Least-privilege IAM for an agent: four actions, no wildcards, with Parameter Store wired for secrets (the token slot exists but v1 runs unauthenticated just fine). SES sandbox is fine when sender and recipient are the same verified address, which skips a production-access request. And the value of a hard line between deterministic logic and model reasoning, so the numbers are trustworthy and the writing is still warm.

sentences.en: Bedrock inference profiles, and why on-demand Nova needs the us.
sentences.zh: 基岩推理概况，以及为什么按需 Nova 需要我们。

sentences.en: prefix and IAM that covers the fan-out regions.
sentences.zh: 前缀和覆盖扇出区域的 IAM。

sentences.en: Least-privilege IAM for an agent: four actions, no wildcards, with Parameter Store wired for secrets (the token slot exists but v1 runs unauthenticated just fine).
sentences.zh: 代理的最低权限 IAM：四个操作，无通配符，参数存储连接用于秘密（令牌槽存在，但 v1 未经身份验证即可正常运行）。

sentences.en: SES sandbox is fine when sender and recipient are the same verified address, which skips a production-access request.
sentences.zh: 当发件人和收件人是相同的经过验证的地址时，SES 沙箱就可以正常工作，从而跳过生产访问请求。

sentences.en: And the value of a hard line between deterministic logic and model reasoning, so the numbers are trustworthy and the writing is still warm.
sentences.zh: 以及确定性逻辑和模型推理之间的硬性界限的价值，因此数字是值得信赖的，文笔仍然是温暖的。

blocks.html_en: The proof it ran without me: I set the EventBridge schedule to fire at 8:07 PM, stepped away, and it went off on its own. It pulled all 49 repos, Nova wrote the brief, and SES delivered it in about 4 seconds, with the CloudWatch timestamp and the email to match. That was a real scheduled trigger, not a button, which is the whole point of the challenge. With autonomy proven, I set the production schedule to 6 AM Pacific daily. The verbatim CloudWatch logs, timestamps, and request IDs are in PROOF.md.

sentences.en: The proof it ran without me: I set the EventBridge schedule to fire at 8:07 PM, stepped away, and it went off on its own.
sentences.zh: 证明它在没有我的情况下运行：我将 EventBridge 时间表设置为晚上 8:07 启动，然后走开，它就自行启动了。

sentences.en: It pulled all 49 repos, Nova wrote the brief, and SES delivered it in about 4 seconds, with the CloudWatch timestamp and the email to match.
sentences.zh: 它提取了所有 49 个存储库，Nova 撰写了摘要，SES 在大约 4 秒内交付了它，并提供了匹配的 CloudWatch 时间戳和电子邮件。

sentences.en: That was a real scheduled trigger, not a button, which is the whole point of the challenge.
sentences.zh: 这是一个真正的预定触发器，而不是一个按钮，这就是挑战的重点。

sentences.en: With autonomy proven, I set the production schedule to 6 AM Pacific daily.
sentences.zh: 自主性得到验证后，我将生产计划设置为太平洋时间每天早上 6 点。

sentences.en: The verbatim CloudWatch logs, timestamps, and request IDs are in PROOF.md.
sentences.zh: 逐字的 CloudWatch 日志、时间戳和请求 ID 位于 PROOF.md 中。

content_html_zh:
愿景和代理人的职责

我建造得很快，而且建造得很多。 截至本周末，共有 49 个公共回购。 我不擅长的是记住他们中的任何一个人的状态。 哪些是实时的，哪些是没有自述文件的，几个月前就沉寂了，然后静静地腐烂了。 过去的答案是我不知道，直到我去挖掘。

天一亮就解决了。 我希望在坐下之前就完成审查：不是我必须记住检查的仪表板，而是我的收件箱中已经存在的摘要，等待黎明时分。 这就是名字。 太平洋时间每天早上 6 点，它会自行醒来，无需按任何按钮。 它会清点我的 GitHub 帐户上的每个存储库，通过 Amazon Nova 对该库存进行分析，并在我的收件箱中留下一个简短的说明：有多少个存储库处于活动状态，有多少个已安静，有多少个缺少自述文件或许可证，以及三个特定的“在此处执行某些操作”提示。 等我醒来的时候，审核已经完成，正在等待。 最好的工具是您永远不需要打开的工具。

它以只读方式运行。 它报告了，我决定。 它永远不会触及我的仓库。

我是如何构建它的

珠三角始终第一。 一个架构文档在一行代码之前带有 MUST、STUB 和 NEVER 标签，然后是逐块构建，每个块后面都有通过或失败检查点。 正常运行时间检查、展示抓取和自动生成的 README 拉取请求都带有实施说明，而不是半成品。 小而精益的运输击败了大而破碎的运输。

我将其作为多模型工作流程运行。 Claude 负责架构和 PRD，Kiro 负责规范驱动的构建。 我编写了阶段感知护栏，以便 Kiro 可以在我工作时进行文件创作和测试，然后切换到上线模式，让它在我回家后使用我的凭据进行部署。 我指导并批准，代理人生成。

值得一提的挑战是让 Nova 做出回答。 我的第一次调用失败，并显示“不支持按需吞吐量”。 Nova 不会根据裸模型 ID 按需运行。 您必须调用跨区域推理配置文件 us.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0，并且您的 IAM 策略必须允许配置文件路由到的每个区域中的配置文件和底层模型。 仅列出配置文件，即使打开了模型访问权限，您也会收到 AccessDenied。 一旦我明白了这一点，它就回答了第一次尝试。

第二节课在构建中进行。 旧的基岩模型访问页面已于 2025 年末停用，亚马逊提供的模型现在已自动启用，因此我计划的步骤已不复存在。 在依赖某个工具之前检查它是否是最新的，这使我免于追逐死控制台页面。

使用的 AWS 服务和架构概述

整个事情就是一个 Lambda 和一个时间表。

  • Amazon EventBridge Scheduler 每天早上 6 点（太平洋时间）通过时区感知的 cron 触发该函数，因此夏令时可以自行处理。 这是触发器，而不是按钮。
  • AWS Lambda 在 Python 中运行代理，第三方依赖项为零，因此部署包是单个压缩文件。
  • Amazon Bedrock 与 Nova Lite 通过 Converse API 进行推理。
  • Amazon SES 通过电子邮件发送简报。
  • Amazon CloudWatch 会记录每次运行并针对任何错误发出警报。

基础设施是单个 AWS SAM 模板，因此整个堆栈作为一个单元进行部署和回滚。

流程：EventBridge Scheduler 触发 Lambda，后者读取 GitHub API、调用 Bedrock Nova，并通过 SES 发送到我的收件箱。

我引以为傲的一个设计选择是：结构是确定性的，风味是生成的。 活跃、安静或未经许可的存储库数量是用 Python 计算的，因此永远不会产生幻觉。 诺瓦只围绕这些固定数字撰写叙述和推动。

我学到了什么

基岩推理概况，以及为什么按需 Nova 需要我们。 前缀和覆盖扇出区域的 IAM。 代理的最低权限 IAM：四个操作，无通配符，参数存储连接用于秘密（令牌槽存在，但 v1 未经身份验证即可正常运行）。 当发件人和收件人是相同的经过验证的地址时，SES 沙箱就可以正常工作，从而跳过生产访问请求。 以及确定性逻辑和模型推理之间的硬性界限的价值，因此数字是值得信赖的，文笔仍然是温暖的。

证明它在没有我的情况下运行：我将 EventBridge 时间表设置为晚上 8:07 启动，然后走开，它就自行启动了。 它提取了所有 49 个存储库，Nova 撰写了摘要，SES 在大约 4 秒内交付了它，并提供了匹配的 CloudWatch 时间戳和电子邮件。 这是一个真正的预定触发器，而不是一个按钮，这就是挑战的重点。 自主性得到验证后，我将生产计划设置为太平洋时间每天早上 6 点。 逐字的 CloudWatch 日志、时间戳和请求 ID 位于 PROOF.md 中。
